channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump pauses tariffs on most nations for 90 days, raises taxes on Chinese imports
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125 percent.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tim Walz says Dems 'should demonize' people like Elon Musk during Ohio town hall
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz criticized Tesla CEO Elon Musk on Wednesday while talking to supporters at an Ohio town hall gathering, according to video footage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent denies that tariff pause is due to market declines
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent denied that the president's move to implement a blanket pause on his tariffs was the result of declines in the financial markets, arguing the action was part of Trump's strategy all along.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 53 international students’ immigration statuses revoked at four more Texas universities
item.description: Nineteen students at the University of Texas at Dallas, 27 at the University of North Texas and others now face uncertainty about their ability to stay in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Ayden Runnels And Jessica Priest, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump-backed bill to stop 'rogue' judges passes House
item.description: House lawmakers advanced a bill to block district judges from issuing nationwide injunctions in most cases.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump orders probe into 'Anonymous' New York Times editorial writer
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday signed a pair of memorandums directing the Department of Justice to investigate two individuals who served in his first administration and pushed back against Trump.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Exclusive: Pence agrees Trump’s tariff policy a potential midterm liability for GOP
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said the Trump administration’s aggressive use of tariffs poses a potential risk for Republicans in the midterm elections, even as he expressed relief about a walk-back of some of the most severe tariffs on allies.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump has a timeline in mind for Iran nuke deal, taps Israel to lead any potential military action
item.description: Trump told reporters Wednesday that if Iran does not give up its nuclear weapons program, military action led by Israel will be a real possibility.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump targets ‘Anonymous’ author and former top cybersecurity official in escalation of retribution
item.description: President Donald Trump is intensifying his efforts to punish his critics by signing a pair of memoranda directing the Justice Department to investigate two officials from his first administration and stripping them of any security clearances they may have.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's 'buy' tip on social media before his tariffs pause made money for investors who listened
item.description: President Donald Trump offered some financial advice on Wednesday morning that ended up making a lot of money for any lucky investor who listened to him.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Federal public health cuts could cost Texas $700 million
item.description: State and local health agencies are having to cut programs and staff to make up the loss and more cuts could be coming.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Terri Langford, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump lets the water flow
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has long complained about modern rules that limit water flow for showerheads, making it harder for him to wash his "beautiful hair.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Options for Trump's space-based 'Golden Dome' missile defense shield head to Hegseth for approval
item.description: U.S. Space Command says it's finalized options for President Donald Trump’s “Golden Dome” missile defense system and has sent its recommendations to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth for his review and approval.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House Republicans and Democrats say the US must maintain its troop totals in Europe
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican and Democratic members of the House Armed Services Committee voiced sharp bipartisan criticism Tuesday about reports the Trump administration is eyeing a reduction of U.S. forces in Europe, saying America must stick with its NATO allies. Rep. Michael Rogers, R-Mich.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: S&P 500 soars 9.5% to one of its biggest gains since WWII after Trump limits tariffs
item.description: Stocks surged to one of their biggest gains since World War II after President Donald Trump paused his tariffs against most other nations, as investors had desperately hoped he would. Trump, though, did raise tariffs further on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kash Patel has been replaced by Army Secretary Driscoll as acting head of the ATF, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — FBI Director Kash Patel has been removed as the acting chief of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives and has been replaced with the Army secretary, three people familiar with the matter said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court allows Trump’s firings of independent agency board members to take effect, for now
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday allowed the Trump administration to oust two board members who oversee independent agencies, for now. The action seems to signal the court's support for President Donald Trump's effort to remove limits on his power to hire and fire.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U of M president outlines efforts to combat antisemitism in wake of White House investigation
item.description: University of Minnesota President Rebecca Cunningham sent a letter to faculty, students and staff underscoring her commitment to preventing antisemitism. A key regional Jewish advocacy organization says the school is making the right moves.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Washington state Democrats want to tax online dating apps
item.description: Dating apps would be required to pay a love tax if a bill in Washington state gets the approval of lawmakers in an effort to fund domestic violence programs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House votes to overturn Biden-era rule limiting bank overdraft fees to $5, sends to Trump to sign
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The House voted Wednesday to overturn a rule that would have limited bank overdraft fees to $5, following the Senate in moving to dismantle the regulation that the Biden administration had estimated would save consumers billions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: CORA LEWIS and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Johnson aiming for budget vote Wednesday, but suggests support is not yet there
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Wednesday that he’s still aiming to stage a vote later in the evening on a Senate-passed budget bill to guide President Trump’s domestic agenda, but suggested the support is not yet there to pass it and the final vote will come later than 5:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House GOP holdouts threaten Trump’s ‘big, beautiful’ budget bill as Speaker Johnson postpones vote
item.description: House Republicans have abruptly postponed a late evening vote on their budget framework as grave misgivings from conservative GOP lawmakers threw it in flux.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Army Secretary Dan Driscoll to lead ATF, replacing FBI Director Kash Patel
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel was replaced by U.S. Army Secretary Dan Driscoll to serve as the acting director of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York bill aims to help homeless people and their pets stay together
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A new state bill aims to expand access to co-sheltering, allowing homeless people and domestic abuse survivors to stay together with their pets during a housing crisis. Senate Bill S673, sponsored by New York state Sen. Monica R.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, PIX 11

item.title: Why Trump faces toughest House GOP test yet on budget blueprint
item.description: Wednesday’s looming House vote on the Senate’s framework for advancing Trump’s legislative agenda is shaping up to be one of the president’s toughest tests yet on Capitol Hill, as hardline Republicans dismiss the White House’s public — and private — entreaties to line up behind the measure that will
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Judges temporarily block 5 Venezuelans' deportations under Alien Enemies Act
item.description: Federal judges in New York and Texas temporarily blocked the Trump administration from deporting five Venezuelan men it alleges are gang members by using the Alien Enemies Act.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: President Trump’s approval rating slipped to its lowest point during his second White House term amid his handling of the economy and the recent Houthi Signal chat leak, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Here's how newly imposed tariffs will affect California products
item.description: President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs could have a profound impact on California’s exports, according to a study from the Public Policy Institute of California.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5
